stem: In a talent pipeline, a candidate begins at 'application'. At the first stage, 38% of cases take Path A (the assessment fast track) and 62% take Path B (the standard route). In Path A, the probability of advancing to the next stage is uncertain, estimated between 52% and 70%. In Path B, the advancement probability is 78%. At the final stage, regardless of path, the probability of successful hire is between 71% and 82% (uncertain due to evaluator variability). What is the overall probability of successful hire?

ground_truth.answer: P(successful hire) = P(A)×P(advance|A)×P(final) + P(B)×P(advance|B)×P(final). Range: [0.484, 0.615]. Point estimate: 0.544.

solution_steps: Total probability via law of total probability over the two paths.
The two sources of uncertainty (Path A advancement and final stage) compound to create the interval [0.484, 0.615].
common_failure_modes: Computing only one path's probability instead of summing over all paths
Not weighting each path by its probability of being taken
Using a single rate for uncertain stages without computing the range
Assuming the paths are independent when they share a common final stage uncertainty
